text: Research-policy dialogues have long been a familiar feature at the national and EU levels. Increasingly they are also a feature at the local level of European cities. However, there is little formal analysis of how municipal politicians and senior officials interact with researchers, or of how researchers attempt to influence urban policy at the local level. This chapter describes the variety of forms, themes and uses of research-policy dialogue at the local level, as well as the factors that influence it. In particular, it explores the degree to which different local developments are shaped by national policy environments and by the EU's increasing role in transferring knowledge from one member-state to another. Our overall conclusion is that EU action has significantly increased the scope for a European-wide, horizontal exchange of best practice by means of targeted research funding and by strengthening European networks of urban municipalities; knowledge-sharing that is less restricted by national particularities.
headline: Fully Human Written
